stdout:
## Success criteria from issue description - **Montgomery internals implemented:** `Fp

` now stores Montgomery form and routes arithmetic through Montgomery helpers (`crates/gf2-core/src/gfp/mod.rs:35, 72, 155, 169, 188`; `crates/gf2-core/src/gfp/montgomery.rs`). - **Axiom/regression coverage appears strong**, including exhaustive small-prime checks and proptests for larger primes (`mod.rs:629-757`, `montgomery.rs:276-425`), but the required **“naive vs Montgomery, 10,000+ random ops across Fp<3>, Fp<65537>, Fp<2^61-1>” is not satisfied as written** (no explicit naive comparator test and no explicit 10k threshold). - **Benchmark success criterion not met:** new benchmark file measures only current `Fp` ops and does not include naive `%` baseline or reported speedup thresholds (`crates/gf2-core/benches/fp_montgomery.rs:7-129`). ## Separation of concerns - `gf2-core` changes are isolated to `crates/gf2-core` (`gfp` + bench + Cargo bench entry), and dependency direction remains clean (no `gf2-coding` dependency added in `crates/gf2-core/Cargo.toml:12-19`). - ✅ Pass. ## Functional paradigm and performance - High-level API remains clean; low-level Montgomery helpers use direct arithmetic and branchless correction where appropriate (`montgomery.rs:74-83, 111-127`). - Constant-time requirement for hot ops is mostly met in implementation style (branchless `redc`, `mont_add`, `mont_sub`; multiply path is direct `redc` for odd `P` in `mod.rs:184-189`). - ❌ Performance acceptance not demonstrated versus naive baseline (missing comparative benchmark evidence). ## Correctness - Field operation behavior is well-covered by exhaustive and property tests (`mod.rs:629-757`, `montgomery.rs:276-425`), and conversion/reduction constants are validated (`montgomery.rs:163-216`). - BitVec tail-masking/bit-numbering invariants are not touched by this change (no regressions observed in affected files). - ✅ No obvious arithmetic invariant break found in reviewed code. ## Testing - Good breadth of tests added (unit + exhaustive + proptest), but the issue’s explicit cross-verification criterion is not fully implemented: no dedicated naive-vs-Montgomery randomized harness at 10k+ operations across all required fields. - Existing gate context shows `fmt`, `clippy`, and `tests` passed, but prior code-review run failed due gate-context invocation error. - ❌ Fail against stated issue success criteria. ## Unsafe isolation - No new `unsafe` introduced in reviewed `gf2-core` changes; unsafe remains isolated by project design. - ✅ Pass. ## Documentation - Public `Fp

` docs were updated with Montgomery semantics and examples (`mod.rs:1-31, 42-71, 90-132`). - Core docs are decent, but this does not compensate for missing benchmark/cross-verification acceptance evidence. - ⚠️ Adequate docs for implementation intent; acceptance docs/evidence incomplete. ## Prior review feedback for this issue - Most recent run failed with: `JIT_CONTEXT_FILE not set. This gate requires --pass-context.` - This operational failure is not demonstrably resolved by the implementation under review (still an unresolved prior-run item). ## Jit issue dependencies - Prerequisite issue `350bff7f` is marked `done` in provided context, and this change correctly builds on an existing `Fp

` implementation. - ✅ Dependency status satisfied. VERDICT: FAIL --- Gate result: FAILED
